title: Sunday, October 22
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
October 22, 2017

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on the Twenty-first Sunday after Pentecost in the liturgical calendar, falling within Ordinary Time. The RCL readings for this date would have been Isaiah 45:1-7, Psalm 96:1-9, 1 Thessalonians 1:1-10, and Matthew 22:15-22 (the passage about rendering unto Caesar). This was approximately one month before the beginning of Advent season.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the final stages of the 79th General Convention, which had concluded just weeks prior in Austin, Texas (late June 2017), so resolutions and decisions from that gathering would still be circulating through dioceses. The Diocese of Colorado was led by Bishop Robert J. O'Neill at this time. There were ongoing national conversations about sexuality and inclusion within the denomination, which had been central to the General Convention discussions.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical late October weather as the season turned toward winter. The Colorado Rockies' baseball season had ended in early October without a playoff appearance. Local political attention was focused on the 2018 midterm election cycle, with Colorado state campaigns beginning to intensify. There were no major weather disasters or immediate crises affecting the Denver metro area during the week of October 16-22.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Las Vegas shooting aftermath dominated national news (the attack occurred October 1, with ongoing coverage of the investigation and policy debates continuing). The Trump administration was engaged in Puerto Rico hurricane recovery efforts, with criticism mounting over the federal response to Hurricanes Irma and Maria. The #MeToo movement was gaining momentum following Harvey Weinstein revelations, with numerous accusations against prominent men in entertainment and media emerging daily. The House Intelligence Committee was conducting Russia investigation hearings with ongoing testimony.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Catalonia was in political crisis following an independence referendum on October 1 that Spain's government had declared illegal, with escalating tensions between regional and national authorities. ISIS was being militarily pressed in Iraq and Syria as coalition forces advanced against remaining strongholds. North Korea tensions remained elevated following months of missile tests and rhetoric, though diplomatic channels were still being explored.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood was tense and fractured, marked by ongoing political polarization, mass shooting trauma, and emerging reckoning with sexual harassment and assault across institutions. The World Series was underway (Houston Astros vs. Los Angeles Dodgers), providing some cultural continuity and distraction for sports audiences. Popular culture and social media were processing rapid institutional and social change, with questions of power, accountability, and inclusion dominating conversations in ways that would resonate within church communities wrestling with similar questions.
